reasoning: The investigation began by analyzing the root cause ranked list, which showed a high concentration of anomalies on the A1 interface, specifically severe hard errors such as packet loss and retransmissions. While the ranked list also indicated anomalies on the F1-c, F1-u, and E2 interfaces, these were primarily volumetric (throughput) or latency-based symptoms with significantly lower anomaly scores (higher distances). Following the Metric Severity Prioritization heuristic, the hard errors on the A1 interface (packet_loss and retransmissions) carry more diagnostic weight than the throughput drops observed on the F1 and E2 interfaces. Furthermore, the A1 interface sits at the highest level of the O-RAN control hierarchy (connecting the Non-RT RIC to the Near-RT RIC). A failure here logically precedes and propagates to the Near-RT RIC, which in turn manages the O-DU and O-CU via the E2 and F1 interfaces. Therefore, the anomalies on E2 and F1 are identified as symptomatic noise resulting from the A1 failure.
